sections.title: STANDARD FOR TABLE GRAPES
sections.text: Adopted in 2007. Amended in 2011.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR TABLE GRAPES
level=1
Adopted in 2007. Amended in 2011.

page_spans.text: Adopted in 2007. Amended in 2011.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of table grapes grown from Vitis vinifera L., of the Vitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Grapes for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of table grapes grown from Vitis vinifera L., of the Vitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Grapes for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of table grapes grown from Vitis vinifera L., of the Vitaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Grapes for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: Maturity Requirements
sections.text:
Table grapes must be sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness.
In order to satisfy this requirement, the fruit must have obtained a refractometric index of at least 16°Brix.
Fruit with a lower refractometric index are accepted provided the sugar/acid ratio is at least equal to:
(a) 20:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 12.5° and less than 14°Brix,
(b) 18:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 14° and less than 16°Brix.

page_spans.text:
Table grapes must be sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness.
In order to satisfy this requirement, the fruit must have obtained a refractometric index of at least 16°Brix.
Fruit with a lower refractometric index are accepted provided the sugar/acid ratio is at least equal to:
(a) 20:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 12.5° and less than 14°Brix,
(b) 18:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 14° and less than 16°Brix.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text:
Table grapes in this class must be of superior quality.
The bunches be characteristic of the variety in shape, development and colouring, allowing for the district in which they are grown.
The berries must be firm, firmly attached to the stalk, evenly spaced along the stalk and have their bloom virtually intact.
They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text:
Table grapes in this class must be of superior quality.
The bunches be characteristic of the variety in shape, development and colouring, allowing for the district in which they are grown.
The berries must be firm, firmly attached to the stalk, evenly spaced along the stalk and have their bloom virtually intact.
They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Table grapes in this class must be of good quality.
The bunches must be characteristic of the variety in shape, development and colouring, allowing for the district in which they are grown.
The berries must be firm, firmly attached to the stalk and, as far as possible, have their bloom intact.
They may, however, be less evenly spaced along the stalk than in the “Extra” Class.

sections.title: Minimum Bunch Weight
sections.text: The minimum bunch weight shall be 75  g. This provision does not apply to packages intended for single servings.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: Ten percent by weight of bunches not satisfying the size requirements as specified in Section 3.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only bunches of the same origin, variety, quality and degree of ripeness. In the “Extra” Class, the bunches must be of more or less identical size and colouring. In the case of Class I, the bunches may have slight variation in size.

In the “Extra” Class, the bunches must be of more or less identical size and colouring. In the case of Class I, the bunches may have slight variation in size.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes table grapes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The bunches may show slight defects in shape, development and colouring, provided these do not impair the essential characteristics of the variety, allowing for the district in which they are grown. The berries must be sufficiently firm and sufficiently attached to the stalk. They may be less evenly spaced along the stalk than in Class I. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the table grapes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: - defects in shape; - defects in colouring; - slight sun scorch affecting the skin only; - slight bruising; - slight skin defects.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Table grapes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Table grapes shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995). In the case of the “Extra” Class, the bunches must be packed in a single layer.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the table grapes. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sentences.original: Table grapes must be sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness.

classification.sentence:
Fruit with a lower refractometric index are accepted provided the sugar/acid ratio is at least equal to:
(a) 20:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 12.5° and less than 14°Brix,
(b) 18:1 if the Brix level is greater than or equal to 14° and less than 16°Brix.
classification.pattern: Declarative sentence granting conditional acceptance with nested numeric conditions and enumerated criteria

components.subject: Fruit with a lower refractometric index
components.condition: provided the sugar/acid ratio is at least equal to enumerated ratios with subconditions

classification.justification: Sentence expresses conditional acceptance with detailed nested quantitative thresholds; structurally more complex than known templates and novel in detail.
notes: Nested numeric conditions with multiple thresholds.

sentences.original: Table grapes in this class must be of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

No known template matches this exact structural pattern, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment.

classification.sentence: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment.
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (must) + verb phrase + list of conditions specifying attributes of the object + alternative location clause

components.subject: Each package
attributes: in letters grouped on the same side
components.alternative_location: or in the documents accompanying the shipment

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation with specific detail on the manner and location of marking that does not closely match any known template, resulting in a new structural pattern reflecting the detailed attribute list and alternative location clause.
notes: The presence of multiple qualifiers and alternative options increases complexity and differentiates this sentence from simpler obligation templates.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause
classification.function: Conditional obligation combined with conditional permission
classification.pattern: If [Condition], [Obligation] and [Permission] on the subject applies

components.Condition: If the produce is not visible from the outside
components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR TABLE GRAPES
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR TABLE GRAPES' com cabeçalho 'CXS 255-2007'. Um único nó estrutural nível 1 com informações adicionais sobre adoção e emenda do padrão.

Página contém cabeçalho com código do documento e numeração de página. Identificados dois títulos de nível 1, sendo o segundo com vários subtítulos até nível 3. Conteúdo abrange definições e provisões de qualidade para uvas de mesa, com continuidade na última subseção e no nó nível 1 principal 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY'.

A página apresenta continuação da seção 2.2.3 Class II iniciada na página anterior, vários níveis de seções estruturadas com títulos numerados e em caixa alta, incluindo as seções principais 3, 4 e 5 e seus respectivos subtítulos. A seção 5 e seu subtítulo 5.1 apresentam continuação na página seguinte. O cabeçalho contém código do documento e numeração da página.

Nesta página, identificam-se as seções 5.2 Packaging e seus subtítulos, além da seção 6 MARKING OR LABELLING com seus subtítulos até o nível 3. O texto associado está corretamente vinculado a cada título. Não há indicação de continuidade da seção 5 nesta página para a próxima, tampouco continuidade da seção 6 da página anterior.

Nesta página foram identificadas as seções 6.2.5, 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE como nível 1, com subtítulos numerados como nível 2. Os títulos e números foram mapeados conforme a numeração visível. O texto apresentado está associado diretamente aos títulos de subtítulos correspondentes, que têm textos descritivos incorporados ao título, portanto text_on_page está vazio. Não há indicações de continuidade para as seções nesta página.
